name: ETH Compression Hold Release v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing a squeeze-release structure that enters only after post-release value hold. The edge should come from broad compression-release participation with realistic asymmetric harvesting rather than buying the first breakout bar blindly.
invalidation_condition: Abort if squeeze-release setups remain too sparse, if release bars instantly mean-revert before hold is visible, or if the exit structure cannot overcome costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression resolves into a directional release, and entry is only taken if price remains aligned with fast value rather than snapping back immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in chop, some compression releases persist for a brief second leg. The goal is to monetize that short-lived persistence with realistic front-loaded exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is a cleaner lane for this than TAO because squeeze and hold structure is easier to read while still offering meaningful range expansion.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If compression resolves straight back into mean reversion, the release will not persist long enough to justify even the front-half targets.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries engage but the hold never extends, keep the entry and compress exits further rather than tightening setup conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Selective entries with a decent first-target hit rate and limited runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h and one confirmation lane show tradable density with positive post-cost continuation after release hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade output, or holds that consistently fail to extend after entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; squeeze and hold are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with moderate compression thresholds instead of extreme squeeze-only definitions

test_lanes.why_included: Best clean lane for release persistence after compression.
test_lanes.continue_if: Release-hold setups trade with credible density and positive post-cost continuation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The setup is too sparse or release holds do not extend.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A modest number of clean squeeze releases with one more leg after the hold.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism generalizes across majors with comparable structure.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC shows similar release-hold behavior with comparable trade density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Only ETH works and BTC does not participate.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Similar but slightly cleaner release-persistence behavior than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same release-hold logic survives on a higher-volatility lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO preserves the mechanism without collapsing into chaos.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO becomes pure noise and invalidates the structure.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger release-persistence events than ETH.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars
